rent.note: furnished studio/1BR in nomad area; tourist town premium over inland Vietnam

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; often bundled in rental

groceries.note: local wet markets; fresh produce very affordable

diningOut.note: mix of local Vietnamese pho/banh mi + tourist-area cafes

transit.note: bicycle rental + occasional Grab; town is small and bike-friendly

coworking.source: digitalnomadindex.com Vietnam guide / local coworking listings
coworking.note: limited coworking options; cafe-based work common; $30-80/mo range

misc.note: massages, entertainment, personal care, day trips, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers applied to RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Raise for peak tourist/weather months, lower for off-peak.

evidence.claim: Hoi An furnished 1BR/studio rent $200-$500/month; coliving private rooms $400-750/month
evidence.quote: rent in smaller towns like Hoi An can be as little as $200 to $400

evidence.claim: Hoi An all-in nomad budget $700-1,100/month comfortable
evidence.quote: a budget of around $800 to $1,100 per month will provide a lovely house or apartment, all utilities, housekeeping, groceries, dining out every day if you choose

evidence.claim: Hoi An has limited coworking; primarily tourist town
evidence.quote: Hoi An, while beautiful, is primarily a tourist town with limited coworking infrastructure—better for short visits than long-term remote work

evidence.claim: Vietnam overall nomad food budget $150-300/month covering home cooking + eating out
